content: @Miguel Ojeda @Wedson Almeida Filho Peter asked me how to find "Linux rust people" on irc ;-)
IRC, so old school. :-)
It has been a while since the last time I used IRC :)
Peter is welcome to send us emails or join the Zulip etc. (Personally, I prefer emails in general).
@Miguel Ojeda Or do you mind joining irc? We are mostly at https://oftc.net/ ;-)

Wei Liu said:

IRC, so old school. :-)

Hey, but irc allows you to use your own client ;-)
What channel?
@Wedson Almeida Filho you can find most core kernel developers at #linux-rt, also #sched, #mm are active from time to time.
Besides, you can do a /whois <nickname> to see which channel the person is at. (That's how I stalked other kernel developers :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes: )

Thanks Boqun!

The /whois trick requires one to know people's nicknames, which I don't :)
So your nick is wedson, right?

yes, I see you're boqun too :)

It looks like I need to be identified and verified, whatever that is, to join #linux-rt and #sched
Yes, some of the channels used to suffer anonymous flooding, so registration is required.

Boqun Feng said:

Hey, but irc allows you to use your own client ;-)

For what is worth, Zulip also allows to use other clients.
They also seem to provide bidirectional bridges for IRC and Matrix.
They also provide a TUI client, as well as other fancy stuff like video through BBB.
I don't have a horse in this race, and I don't mind one or another, but it seems quite complete.

Miguel Ojeda said:

They also seem to provide bidirectional bridges for IRC and Matrix.

I tried to look a bit into the matrix bridge (as matrix is my preferred client). But It looked to me like a server admin has to set it up on the zulip site, and it can only bridge streams that where configured previously.
Matrix refers to matrix.org there, i.e. the network, not a client.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Matrix refers to matrix.org there, i.e. the network, not a client.

Meant that. My main chat protocol is matrix, so the element client

Boqun Feng said:

Hey, but irc allows you to use your own client ;-)

which irc client do you like to use?

Leandro Coutinho said:

Boqun Feng said:

Hey, but irc allows you to use your own client ;-)

which irc client do you like to use?

I use weechat.
Boqun Feng has marked this topic as resolved.
